title: Gas and Food Prices Before July Fourth

source.title: Gas and food prices for 4th of July

segments.title: Holiday Weekend
segments.audioText: With the Fourth of July holiday weekend approaching, travelers are watching prices at the pump and at the grocery store.
segments.transcript: With the Fourth of July holiday weekend approaching, travelers are watching prices at the pump and at the grocery store.
segments.translationZh: 隨住七月四日長週末就快到，旅客都留意緊油站同超市嘅價格。

points.label: holiday weekend approaching
points.explanationZh: approaching 即『就快到』；聽新聞時常用嚟帶出即將發生嘅事。

points.label: prices at the pump
points.explanationZh: at the pump 指入油時嘅油價；pump 係油站入油機。

points.label: at the grocery store
points.explanationZh: 即喺超市；同 at the pump 一齊聽，就係講兩類生活開支。

segments.title: Gas Prices
segments.audioText: Gas prices have been falling, but they are still higher than drivers paid last summer.
segments.transcript: Gas prices have been falling, but they are still higher than drivers paid last summer.
segments.translationZh: 油價一直有回落，但仍然高過司機上個夏天所支付嘅價錢。

points.label: have been falling
points.explanationZh: 現在完成進行式表示最近一段時間一直下降；聽到 have been 要留意後面動詞。

points.label: still higher than
points.explanationZh: still higher than 即『仍然高過』，用嚟比較而且帶少少『未完全改善』嘅意思。

points.label: drivers paid
points.explanationZh: drivers paid 係『司機曾經付嘅價錢』；paid 呢度係過去式。

segments.title: Food Budget
segments.audioText: Food prices are also part of the holiday budget, especially for families planning cookouts.
segments.transcript: Food prices are also part of the holiday budget, especially for families planning cookouts.
segments.translationZh: 食物價格亦係節日預算一部分，特別係打算燒烤聚餐嘅家庭。

points.label: part of the holiday budget
points.explanationZh: part of... 即『……嘅一部分』；budget 係預算。

points.label: especially for families
points.explanationZh: especially for 用嚟點出受影響最大或最相關嘅對象。

points.label: planning cookouts
points.explanationZh: cookout 係美式英文，通常指戶外燒烤或戶外聚餐。

segments.title: Saving Money
segments.audioText: Experts say shopping early and comparing prices can help families save money.
segments.transcript: Experts say shopping early and comparing prices can help families save money.
segments.translationZh: 專家話，早啲買同比較價格，可以幫家庭慳錢。

points.label: Experts say
points.explanationZh: 新聞常用 Experts say 帶出建議或分析；聽到 say 後面通常係重點。

points.label: shopping early
points.explanationZh: shopping early 即早啲買，避免臨急先買時選擇少或價錢高。

points.label: comparing prices
points.explanationZh: compare prices 係格價；日常生活好實用。

sentences: With the Fourth of July holiday weekend approaching, travelers are watching prices at the pump and at the grocery store.
Gas prices have been falling, but they are still higher than drivers paid last summer.
Food prices are also part of the holiday budget, especially for families planning cookouts.
Experts say shopping early and comparing prices can help families save money.

vocabulary.example: Travelers are checking gas prices before the trip.
vocabulary.translationZh: 出行人士出發前查緊油價。

vocabulary.example: Prices at the pump can change quickly.
vocabulary.translationZh: 油站油價可以變得好快。

vocabulary.example: Grocery prices affect every family budget.
vocabulary.translationZh: 食品價格影響每個家庭預算。

vocabulary.example: We need to stay within our holiday budget.
vocabulary.translationZh: 我哋要控制喺節日預算之內。

vocabulary.example: They are planning a cookout for the holiday.
vocabulary.translationZh: 佢哋打算假期搞戶外燒烤聚餐。

phrases.example: Drivers are paying more at the pump this week.
Families notice higher prices at the grocery store.
Food is a big part of the holiday budget.
Shopping early can help you save money.

speakingPractice.promptZh: 由 00:06 開始，先聽一次，再跟讀 travelers are watching prices at the pump 呢個 chunk。
重播 00:24，寫低 Gas prices have been falling 呢半句，再對稿改正。
用 Experts say... can help families save money. 造一句：Experts say planning ahead can help families save money.
用 2 句英文講返重點：holiday costs、gas prices、food prices。

notes: 先盲聽四個時間點，唔好一開始就睇稿，訓練自己抓關鍵詞。
聽比較句時留意 still higher than，than 後面通常係比較基準。
跟讀時將 at the pump / at the grocery store 當兩個固定地點 chunk。
最後用 30 秒口頭總結：Travelers are watching gas and food prices before July Fourth.

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking Grocery Prices
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同朋友去超市，想買燒烤食材但又想控制預算。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Do we need this brand? This one is a little cheaper. Let's compare prices before we grab everything. Good call. We can still make it work.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 我哋一定要買呢個牌子嗎？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: This one is a little cheaper.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Let's compare prices before we grab everything.
lines.zh: 我哋拎齊晒之前先格一格價。

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Good call. We can still make it work.
lines.zh: 好主意。都仲可以搞得掂。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Good call 係好自然嘅美式口語，意思係『好決定／好主意』，語氣輕鬆又肯定對方。

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 用 This one is a little... 換不同形容詞。
drills.example: This one is a little cheaper / fresher / smaller.

drills.label: quick repeat
drills.prompt: 連續講三次 Let's compare prices before we grab everything.
drills.example: Let's compare prices before we grab everything.

dailyLifePractice.title: Planning a Cookout
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同鄰居傾週末燒烤安排，想禮貌咁分擔食物。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Are you still doing the cookout this weekend? Yeah, but we're keeping it simple. I can bring chips or drinks. That would help a lot.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Are you still doing the cookout this weekend?
lines.zh: 你今個週末仲會搞燒烤嗎？

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yeah, but we're keeping it simple.
lines.zh: 會呀，不過我哋會簡單啲。

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: I can bring chips or drinks.
lines.zh: 我可以帶薯片或者飲品。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: We're keeping it simple 聽起來自然又唔失禮，表示唔想搞太大陣仗，適合朋友、鄰居、同事之間講。

drills.label: role-play
drills.prompt: 你做 A，主動提出帶一樣食物或飲品。
drills.example: I can bring chips or drinks.

drills.label: say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 keeping it simple 講一個你嘅週末安排。
drills.example: We're keeping it simple and ordering pizza.

dailyLifePractice.title: Splitting Gas
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同朋友共乘去短途旅行，想自然咁提出分擔油錢。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Thanks for driving today. Let me chip in for gas. You don't have to. No, seriously, it's only fair.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Let me chip in for gas.

lines.speaker: A
lines.zh: 唔係，真係應該嘅，咁先公平。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: chip in for gas 係好地道講法，意思係分擔油錢；No, seriously 可以令語氣真誠，但唔會太硬。

drills.label: polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 Let me chip in for... 換成其他開支。
drills.example: Let me chip in for parking.

drills.label: short response practice
drills.prompt: 對方話 You don't have to. 你用一句自然回應。
